behavior_in_task_description.reason: All asserted paths (skills/supersede-pr/SKILL.md, scripts/close_superseded_pr.ts, tsconfig.json, skills/make-pr/SKILL.md, AGENTS.md), literal strings ('numeric'/'number', 'different', '--repo', '--dry-run', 'close_superseded_pr' or 'old_pr', shebang '#!/usr/bin/env node'), and schema keys (strict, noEmit) are explicitly mentioned in instruction.md.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes requirements (validation, flags, help output) but doesn't give the implementation code.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests check behavior (exit codes, stderr keywords, flag presence) and config keys, not specific variable names or internal helpers.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests execute the script via subprocess with various args and run pnpm lint/typecheck/tests; they invoke code rather than just grep files.

test_not_tautological.reason: Each f2p test requires actual behavior (script must parse, validate, emit specific messages, exit codes); a stub returning default wouldn't satisfy the validation + help assertions.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Instruction requires AGENTS.md updates (code style rule for one-off TS scripts, Skills section), make-pr SKILL.md, YAML frontmatter with name/description, and agents/openai.yaml files, but the visible test file has empty config_edit test bodies (only comments) so these behaviors aren't actually tested.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with section comments (pass_to_pass gates, fail_to_pass behavioral, config_edit) and descriptive names.

structured_data_schema.reason: tsconfig schema keys (strict, noEmit) and SKILL.md frontmatter fields (name, description) are documented in instruction.md.

no_typos.reason: Paths and flags are consistent between instruction and tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction describes files to create at appropriate granularity without over-specifying implementation lines.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: The config_edit test functions in test_outputs.py are empty placeholders (only '# [config_edit] fail_to_pass' comments with no assertions), so although instruction requires AGENTS.md edits, no test actually validates the scope/content of those edits.
